data.azimuth: Azimuth (360 degrees, 0 = North, 90 = East, 180 = South, 270 = West)
data.modules_power: Total Watt peak power of your solar modules

user.description: Fill in the data of your solar panels. Please refer to the documentation if a field is unclear.

abort.api_key_required: An API key is required to add more than one plane. You can configure it in the integration options.
abort.max_planes: You can add a maximum of 4 planes.

user.description: Add a solar plane. Multiple planes are supported with a Forecast.Solar API subscription.

api_key_required.message: An API key is required when more than one plane is configured

end_before_start.message: The end time must be after the start time.

no_plane.message: No plane configured, cannot set up Forecast.Solar

error.api_key_required: An API key is required to add more than one plane. You can configure it in the integration options.

data.damping_evening: Damping factor: adjusts the results in the evening
data.damping_morning: Damping factor: adjusts the results in the morning

init.description: These values allow tweaking the Forecast.Solar result. Please refer to the documentation if a field is unclear.

options.raw: Native (15-minute or hourly, depending on account)

get_forecast.description: Returns the solar production forecast time series for a configured Forecast.Solar entry.

config_entry.description: The Forecast.Solar configuration entry to query.
config_entry.name: Forecast.Solar entry

end.description: Latest period start to return (exclusive). If omitted, the response includes the full cached forecast.
end.name: End

resolution.description: Resolution of the returned series. 'Native' uses the upstream Forecast.Solar resolution (15-minute on paid accounts, hourly on the free tier). 'Hourly' aggregates to whole-hour buckets.
resolution.name: Resolution

start.description: Earliest period start to return (inclusive). If omitted, the response begins at the start of the cached forecast.
start.name: Start

get_forecast.name: Get forecast
